subject: Biology
title: Eusocial Insects: Ant and Bee Colonies
description: A quick, practical guide to Eusocial Insects: Ant and Bee Colonies.

hook.text: In a honeybee colony, 60,000 bees share one mother. Most will never reproduce.

buildup.text: Eusocial insects have a queen who reproduces, workers who labor, and soldiers who defend.

discovery.text: Workers sacrifice their own reproduction to raise sisters who share 75% of their genes.

twist.text: Leafcutter ants farm fungus gardens underground—the only animals besides humans that practice agriculture.

climax.text: A single ant colony can contain millions of individuals acting as a superorganism.

punchline.text: Millions of workers. One queen. Zero debate.

quiz.question: What do leafcutter ants uniquely do?
